subject: Nephrology
question: A 65-year-old diabetic woman has eGFR 28 mL/min, urine albumin 450 mg/g. What is her CKD stage and most important management?

options.text: Stage 4 CKD - ACEi/ARB + prepare for RRT
options.explanation: eGFR 28 = Stage 4 (15-29). Start ACEi/ARB for proteinuria (A3: >300 mg/g). Nephrology referral. Prepare for renal replacement therapy: AV fistula when eGFR <20. Anemia and bone management.
eGFR 28 is Stage 4, not Stage 3 (30-59). Stage 4 requires more aggressive management including RRT preparation.
Stage 5 = eGFR <15 or dialysis. At eGFR 28, dialysis not yet indicated. Prepare (AV fistula) but don't start until eGFR <15 or uremic symptoms.
Stage 2 = eGFR 60-89 with kidney damage markers. This is Stage 4 requiring intensive management.
eGFR 28 is severely reduced. This patient needs immediate nephrology referral and CKD management.

educationalObjective: Stage CKD by eGFR and albuminuria. Stage 4 requires nephrology referral, RRT preparation, and complication management.
keyTakeaway: CKD Stage 4: eGFR 15-29. ACEi/ARB for proteinuria. AV fistula when eGFR <20. Manage anemia, bone disease, acidosis.
